description:
Trigger: Build secretx-desktop on a target other than linux/macos/windows (e.g. iOS, Android, FreeBSD without sync-secret-service feature path matching).
Expected: backend either fails to compile or returns a clear 'unsupported platform' error.
Actual: keyring v3 silently falls back to its in-process 'mock' credential store on unsupported targets. The inventory!() submissions in lib.rs:173 and lib.rs:181 are unconditional, so secretx:desktop:... URIs resolve and 'work' against an in-memory mock — secrets appear to write and read, but are not persisted and not accessible across processes.

Compare: secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:183 and :192 gate inventory::submit! on #[cfg(target_os = "linux")] precisely to avoid this footgun.

Fix: gate both inventory::submit! calls on #[cfg(any(target_os = "linux", target_os = "macos", target_os = "windows"))] (or add freebsd/openbsd if Secret Service support there is intended). Add a compile-time message or runtime InvalidUri on other targets.

Impact: a binary cross-compiled to iOS that uses secretx:desktop URIs reports successful credential storage that vanishes on process exit. Silent data loss.
title: Inventory registration unconditional; iOS/Android/etc fall back to keyring's mock store
